rulings.Case Title: ALLYSYN OVERTON VS. PATRICK LAKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton For Relief From And To Set Aside Entry Of Dismissal Pursuant To Ccp 473;
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2014, Line 6: CROSS COMPLAINANT PATRICK LAKE's Motion For Relief From And To Set Aside Entry Of Dismissal Pursuant To Ccp 473: GRANTED based on mandatory provision of CCP 473(b). March 20, 2014 Order sustaining demurer without leave to amend is vacated and the new order sustaining the demurer with leave to amend shall be entered. Cross-complainant shall have until June 20, 2014 to file and serve an amended cross-complaint. Cross-complainant's counsel shall pay $550 to Plaintiff's counsel within 10 days of entry the order. Moving party shall present the order granting this motion and the order sustaining demurer with leave to amend to the Court at the hearing. = (501/REQ)

Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANTS PAYMON GHAFOURI, GENEE FONSECA, D MARKET MASTERS INC, GHAFOURI INC, PAYMON INC., and D MARKET's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Sustained with leave to amend to either join Nazanin Bakhodari or allege facts supporting Plaintiff's assertion that any rights or liabilities in this case flow to the community estate. Plaintiff shall have 10 days form the notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: AURORA KING et al VS. HYDE STREET HOLDING COMPANY, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 19, 2014, Line 8: DEFENDANT HYDE STREET HOLDING COMPANY LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer based on misjoinder is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to (1) join the prior owner; or (2) allege facts showing that Hyde is liable for prior owner's wrongdoings on each cause of action; or (3) limit the allegations/damages to Hyde's ownership. Demurrer based on the statute of limitations is overruled. Applying the theories of continuous accrual and equitable tolling, Plaintiffs' claims have been brought within the applicable statues of limitations, i.e. last accrual date is November 2012 and claims have been brought on May 24, 2013. As to the demurrer based on the pending action, Plaintiffs may, if proper, join Meridian as a defendant in this action in Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint. Otherwise this action shall be abated until Zhigadlo v. Meridian is resolved. Plaintiffs shall file amended complaint within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Moving party prepare order. = (501/REQ)
